symptoms.symptom_name: Multiple seizures without return to baseline between episodes

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Seizure cluster at risk of status epilepticus
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Defining feature distinguishing a dangerous cluster from isolated self-terminating seizures.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active facial bleeding from a laceration

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Traumatic facial laceration secondary to drop attack
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct consequence of the atonic seizure/fall.

symptoms.symptom_name: Availability of prescribed rescue/emergency seizure medication

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms this is an anticipated emergency scenario for which a bridging treatment exists.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound post-ictal lethargy not tracking visually, flaccid tone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective confirmation beyond baseline; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute seizure cluster in Lennox-Gastaut syndrome
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Repeated seizures without return to baseline, a missed AED dose, and facial trauma from the initial drop attack together confirm a dangerous seizure cluster requiring urgent rescue medication and emergency transport.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate administration of prescribed rescue benzodiazepine (buccal/intranasal midazolam) per individualised action plan
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line treatment to abort an evolving seizure cluster before it progresses to status epilepticus.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Paediatric emergency department assessment including wound care and consideration of CT head for the traumatic injury
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required given ongoing seizure risk and traumatic facial injury from the fall.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Waiting at home to see if the seizures stop on their own without giving rescue medication
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would risk progression to status epilepticus and delay treatment of the facial injury.
